evals.prompt: I have an sdd.md and spec.json for a loan approval case. Generate the implementation tasks from these spec files so I can build it.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces a tasks.md with ordered task entries covering case creation, stages, tasks, and conditions, then writes caseplan.json directly after user approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
References uip maestro case registry pull for upfront caching
Produces a tasks.md file with numbered task sections (T01, T02, etc.)
Includes registry lookups to resolve taskTypeIds
Generates a registry-resolved.json for audit
Asks user for approval before executing the build

evals.prompt: Break down my case spec into implementation tasks. The spec files are at ./specs/sdd.md and ./specs/spec.json
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, reads both spec files, resolves registry lookups, generates tasks.md, and executes after approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Reads both sdd.md and spec.json from the provided paths
Uses the component_type mapping to determine registry search types
Produces a tasks/ folder with tasks.md
Waits for user approval before proceeding to execution

evals.prompt: Convert this case specification into a step-by-step plan and build the case
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces an actionable tasks.md, then writes caseplan.json directly via the matching plugins after user approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Produces tasks.md with declarative task entries (no shell commands in tasks.md)
Cross-references use human-readable format like Stage.Task.field
Writes caseplan.json directly via plugin impl-json.md recipes after user approves tasks.md

evals.prompt: I want to create a case management project
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and asks the user for the sdd.md design document to proceed
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Asks user for the sdd.md file path

evals.prompt: Add a stage called 'Document Review' to my case.stage.json file
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger case-management planning workflow -- this is a direct stage edit on an existing case file
expectations: The case-management skill may be triggered for direct editing
Does NOT go through the sdd.md planning workflow

evals.prompt: Generate the case from ./sdd.md using v20 schema
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, detects v20 trigger phrase in activating message per Rule 18, writes Schema: v20 header into tasks.md, emits caseplan.json with v20 top-level shape (id: case-<10>, version: 20.0.0, metadata, top-level bindings/variables, layout: {}), runs Phase 4 validate authoritatively (CLI now accepts the v20 shape)
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Schema header in tasks.md reads exactly 'Schema: v20'
caseplan.json top-level keys are: id, version, name, metadata, bindings, variables, nodes, edges, layout
caseplan.json.version === '20.0.0'
caseplan.json.id matches /^case-[A-Za-z0-9]{10}$/
No 'root' wrapper key in caseplan.json
metadata.caseExitRules is used (NOT root.caseExitConditions) once case-exit conditions are written
metadata.slaRules is used for root-target SLA (NOT root.data.slaRules)
Top-level bindings and variables (NOT root.data.uipath.bindings or root.data.uipath.variables)
Stage and Trigger nodes have NO position, style, measured, width, height, zIndex (Rule 19)
Top-level layout: {} present and empty
Phase 4 validate runs authoritatively — same 3-retry cap and hard stop as v19
Phase 5/6 prompts include the v20 downstream-may-reject warning lines

evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, no v20 phrase in activating message, defaults to v19 emit per Rule 18
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Schema header in tasks.md reads exactly 'Schema: v19'
caseplan.json has root wrapper with root.version === 'v19'
Phase 4 retry-cap hard stop active (full v19 behavior)
No v20 warning lines printed at Phase 5/6
